author: Sampuran Das, Pandit, Vaidyaraj
title: Granth Megh Binod (Muni Megh Rej Rachit) ka Sukh Bodhna Tika, Bhag 1
gloss: A commentary on 'Megh Binod,' a book on Vaidic medicine.
copyright: Sant Sampuran Das, Patiala State; No. 1, dated the 7th February, 1910.
notes: 690-page Ayurvedic commentary. Copyright No. 1 of the year.

title: Anand Vivah Sahaik Fund de Sankhep ate Sadharan Samachar
gloss: Prospectus of the Marriage Fund started for the exclusive benefit of the Sikhs.
publisher: B. Teja Singh, Honorary Secretary, Sikh Parivar Sahaik Deg, Gujránwála
notes: Prospectus of a Sikh-only mutual marriage fund — communal-boundary mutual aid tied to the Anand Marriage Act (p8 s1).

title: Danke di Chot
gloss: A price list of the medicines prepared by the Gobind Aushudhalaya, Lahore.
copyright: The Manager, Gobind Aushadhalaya, Lahore; No. 7, dated the 4th March, 1910.
notes: Ayurvedic pharmacy price-list ('Beat of the Drum') — commercial catalog registered and copyrighted as a book.

author: Dilail Singh, Bhai
title: Tara Puara
gloss: A small pamphlet in which the author condemns the custom according to which a Hindu wife, reaching her husband's house when the planet Venus is hidden from view, has to pass the night on which it re-appears in a temple or inn.
notes: Reform tract against the 'tara' (Venus) custom delaying a bride's entry — Sikh-reformist critique of a Hindu marriage superstition.

author: Kesar Singh, Bhai, Translator
title: Japan di Istri Sikhiya
gloss: A treatise on the importance of female education and its good results in Japan.
notes: 'Female Education in Japan' — Meiji Japan as a modernization model for Punjabi female education; the pan-Asian reform gaze (cf. the Young Turk / Awakening of Turkey interest in Q4).

author: Labh Singh, Bhai
title: Central Khalsa Yatimkhana de Maujuda halat da Asli Photo arthat Ashram di vigar chuki dasha de sudhar lai Guru Panth pas binai
gloss: An exposure of the present state of the Central Orphanage of the Chief Khalsa Diwan.
notes: A whistle-blowing 'real photo' exposé of mismanagement at the Chief Khalsa Diwan's central orphanage — internal Sikh institutional accountability polemic (orphanages were the shuddhi demographic front).

author: Narayan Singh, Dr., and Jagat Singh, Bhai
title: Sau Sala Jantri, Bhag Pahla
gloss: A hundred years' calendar, with brief notes on the margin describing the good done by the Sikh Gurus, P. 1.
copyright: Dr. Narayan Singh, Hospital Assistant, Lyallpur; No. 12, dated the 28th January, 1910.
notes: A 100-year calendar broadside with Sikh-Guru marginalia — almanac genre (the year's mass medium) meets Sikh didactics. No page count (single sheet).
